data_sources: Granola (GLOC, CLS Health, Plymouth Bay Ortho, OS1 — meeting notes recovered)

excluded_deals.name: Remix Medical
excluded_deals.reason: Closed out 4/7 — unresponsive since Feb 25 across multiple follow-ups; Osvaldo was not the decision maker, providers never engaged

excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America
excluded_deals.reason: Solv passed 3/31 — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility; outside ICP; price-sensitive

excluded_deals.name: Plymouth Bay Ortho
excluded_deals.reason: No-go confirmed 4/22 (Granola) — ECW build too substantial for a single urgent care location; HMO referral workflow unsupported; Teresa and Brantley passed

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.75/visit (ClearPay) at scale; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition; ~2K calls/week; starting with 2 locations
deals.status_narrative: GLOC signed the agreement on 4/27 — first true outbound Monarch win. Jim (CEO) drove it across the finish line after a multi-week pricing and ROI process. Pricing locked: free during pilot, then $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.75/visit (ClearPay) at scale. Katie and Maria finalized the contract 4/24; Salesforce update was in progress at signing. Internal EPD kickoff with Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, and Carli is being scheduled. In-person design partner collaboration in Michigan planned.
key_risks: Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
No ortho reference customer — Jim flagged this throughout but softened over time
Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity elevated
deals.next_step: Schedule internal EPD kickoff (Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, Carli) then external first meeting with Jim. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
deals.key_notes_one_line: SIGNED 4/27; first Monarch win; internal EPD kickoff scheduling in progress.

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/visit ClearPay; ~250 providers (scaling to 1,000); Texas; 15–30 providers and ~15–30 telemed visits/day to start
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager, technical lead); Ammar Baba (Director, exec sponsor); CLS President engaged and applying internal pressure
deals.status_narrative: Two-part deal as of Teresa's 4/28 clarification: (1) UC Telemed — green if Solv does not need to read the schedule; (2) Specialties — Kapeesh scoping call needed. Ideal outcome: sign UC telemed + design partner for specialties. Acceptable fallback: UC telemed only. CLS came back 4/24 with pricing asks framed as 'wanting to make it easier to sign': per-encounter or hybrid pricing, 6-month initial term vs. 1 year, waived $2K implementation fee. ECW + SolvPay compatibility is unconfirmed and gates Part 1 entirely. Luma confirmed it cannot do dynamic eligibility; pVerify cannot handle auth/referral workflows — Solv has a clear competitive opening. Kapeesh had a Friday (4/25 or 5/2) call with Director Ammar Baba. Target launch: June 1.
key_risks: ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — this single question gates the entire Part 1 green-light
Pricing concession stack (hybrid pricing, 6-month term, waived impl fee) needs finance sign-off and compresses margin
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
deals.next_step: Teresa to confirm whether Solv needs to read schedule for ECW telemed (gates Part 1, ASAP). Katie/Teresa to finalize pricing model and respond to CLS counter-requests. Kapeesh to run specialties design partner scoping call. Owner: Teresa/Katie/Kapeesh. Due: EOW 5/2.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Two-part deal (UC telemed + specialties design partner); ECW+SolvPay gate unresolved; pricing counter-requests open.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused — was near Paper Process)
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating; Dr. Basmajian (CEO, ultimate DM — never directly engaged by Solv)
deals.status_narrative: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — 'our hands are tied at the moment' — with no timeline given. Deal was at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign ready to send, 3-phase contract structure modeled at ~$0.60–$1.00/resolved call for ~20K calls/month) before the Assort blocker surfaced 2/25. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv. Brantley on a passive ~2-week check-in cadence; last touch targeted ~4/14 is now 2+ weeks overdue.
key_risks: Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path forward
Check-in now 2+ weeks overdue; if Assort resolves and a competitor has stayed active, deal is at risk
Dr. Basmajian (ultimate DM) has never been directly engaged by Solv
deals.next_step: Brantley to send check-in today — targeted 4/14 cadence is 2+ weeks overdue. Passive monitoring only; do not push. Owner: Brantley. Due: Today.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Assort contract hard block since 3/11; Jon intends to return when clear; check-in 2+ weeks overdue.

deals.name: Total Primary Care
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Cut Off — 6–9 month hold)
deals.size: 47 locations confirmed; ECW; CTO-built scheduler locked in; acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
deals.status_narrative: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC's CTO-built scheduler is locked in — 'too many resources poured into it' — and will not be replaced. Rikin wants Solv as a patient acquisition layer only (GMB routing to Solv booking, running in parallel with their home-built scheduler) with ECW sync for new bookings only. Not on their timeline for 6–9 months. Actively piloting an unnamed competing voice agent (Rikin declined to name it). Non-standard Monarch deal structure; team accepted the shutdown offer.
key_risks: 6–9 month self-stated hold with no commitment; long, low-probability path
Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — Solv product appetite for this model undefined
Competing voice agent pilot underway with unknown outcome
deals.next_step: Internal decision needed: park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or formally close out. If pursuing, validate acquisition-layer + ECW integration feasibility first. Owner: Teresa / Kapeesh. Due: Now.
deals.next_step_timing: Decision needed now; re-engage Q3 2026 if parked
deals.key_notes_one_line: Cut off 4/7; won't replace scheduler; 6–9 month hold; must decide to park or close.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.deal_type: Expansion (inferred — Dr. Tovmassian also owns Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care)
deals.size: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only
deals.champion: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit product-fit objection
deals.status_narrative: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner) pushed back explicitly on 3/16: 'This doesn't make sense for our practice. 90% of our patients are recurring and their insurances are stable.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate model does not fit a high-recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-up requests for a leadership call since 3/24 — dark for 5+ weeks. Real collection pain exists (post-visit statements, IPA routing errors causing downstream billing issues) but the product-fit gap is owner-level and genuine. Team is not pushing hard given Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care.
key_risks: Product-fit objection is explicit and owner-level — not a pricing or timing issue
Dark since mid-March; 5+ weeks of ghosting
Aggressive pursuit risks the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership
deals.next_step: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Re-engage only if ClearPay model evolves to better fit recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Owner's explicit product-fit objection since 3/16; near-dead; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.

early_stage_watch.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
early_stage_watch.stage: Prospecting — board internal meeting ~4/29–4/30; reconnect with Brantley this week
early_stage_watch.contact: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former CEO of AFC)
early_stage_watch.notes: Post-UCA warm intro via Heather at happy hour. First substantive call 4/24 with Brantley. Urgent ortho model; 4 locations. Cash-constrained: opened 3 locations at once; Atlanta/Kennesaw location struggling badly (opened months without an MRI, hurt volume and revenue). Randy was meeting with OS1 team ~4/29–4/30 to assess appetite for reviewing solutions. Pricing call imminent or completed. Evan Cory preparing Maya and ClearPay collateral; Olivia's new ClearPay landing page identified as a strong resource for the board deck. Reconnect with Brantley expected this week (~5/1–5/5). Cash-strapped flag: could kill the deal before it starts. Monthly board cadence flagged as slow.

executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Closed Won | Design Partner
- **Size**: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.75/visit (ClearPay); 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs; ~2K calls/week
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jim Stilley (CEO)
- **Status**: GLOC signed on 4/27 — first true outbound Monarch win. Pricing locked as usage-based at scale after free pilot. Contract finalized by Katie/Maria; Salesforce update in progress. Internal EPD kickoff with Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, and Carli being scheduled; external kickoff with Jim to follow. In-person collaboration in Michigan planned.
- **Key risks**:
  - Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
  - No ortho reference customer — Jim raised this but softened
  - Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity is elevated
- **Next step**: Schedule internal EPD kickoff and external kickoff with Jim. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Paper Process | New Logo + Design Partner
- **Size**: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay; ~250 providers total; 15–30 visits/day to start
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops); Ammar Baba (Director, exec); CLS President engaged
- **Status**: Teresa defined two-part structure 4/28: Part 1 is UC Telemed (green if Solv doesn't need to read the schedule), Part 2 is Specialties design partner (Kapeesh scoping call needed). CLS came back 4/24 with pricing asks: per-encounter or hybrid model, 6-month first term, waived $2K impl fee. ECW+SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gates Part 1. Luma and pVerify both confirmed they can't solve CLS's auth/referral needs; Solv has a clear lane. Kapeesh call with Director Ammar Baba scheduled.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — single question gates Part 1 entirely
  - Pricing concession stack compresses margin; needs finance sign-off
  - EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term flexibility
- **Next step**: Teresa to confirm ECW telemed schedule-read question (ASAP). Katie/Teresa to send revised pricing model. Kapeesh to complete director scoping call. Owner: Teresa/Katie/Kapeesh. Due: EOW 5/2.
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and proactive
- **Status**: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — no timeline. Was near-Paper Process before this (3-phase contract modeled, DocuSign ready). Jon confirmed intent to return when clear. Check-in cadence was ~every 2 weeks; last touch targeted ~4/14 is now 2+ weeks overdue.
- **Key risks**:
  - Assort exit fully outside our control — no active path forward
  - Check-in 2+ weeks overdue; competitive exposure if Assort resolves and another vendor is active
  - Dr. Basmajian (ultimate DM) has never been engaged by Solv
- **Next step**: Brantley to send check-in today. Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley. Due: Today.
executive_summaries.total_primary_care:
**Total Primary Care** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Cut Off) | New Logo
- **Size**: 47 locations; ECW; acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no executive sponsor
- **Status**: Cut off 4/7. CTO-built scheduler is locked in and will not be replaced. Rikin wants Solv as acquisition layer only, not on timeline for 6–9 months. Competing voice agent pilot underway. Non-standard Monarch deal; team accepted shutdown.
- **Key risks**:
  - 6–9 month hold with zero commitment; low-probability
  - Acquisition-layer scope is non-standard; Solv appetite undefined
  - Competing voice agent pilot active
- **Next step**: Internal Solv decision — park for Q3 2026 or formally close. Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Now.
executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (Admin, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit objection
- **Status**: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back explicitly on 3/16 — '90% of patients recurring, doesn't make sense.' ClearPay's model doesn't fit high-recurring primary care. Dark since mid-March; 5+ weeks of no response. Real pain exists on collections but product fit doesn't. Pursuing risks the Mederi Urgent Care relationship.
- **Key risks**:
  - Owner-level product-fit objection — not pricing or timing
  - 5+ weeks of ghosting
  - Pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership
- **Next step**: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Owner: Brantley.

leadership_table.key_notes: SIGNED 4/27; first Monarch win; kickoff scheduling this week.
ECW+SolvPay compatibility unresolved; pricing counter-requests open; two-part deal structure.
Assort contract hard block since 3/11; Jon intends to return; check-in 2+ weeks overdue.
Cut off 4/7; won't replace scheduler; team must decide to park or formally close.
Owner's explicit product-fit objection; near-dead; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.
Post-UCA board intro; OS1 internal meeting ~4/29; reconnect imminent; budget is a real risk.
